description: Drive a WLED LED strip from video content playing in Jellyfin
name: WLED TV
overview: Samples pixel colours along the edges of the playing video and sends them to a WLED controller in real time, creating an immersive edge-lighting effect that reacts to the content on screen.

versions.changelog: Fix last LED batch ignored by WLED when strip length is not a multiple of 54. The final partial batch is now padded with black (000000) to match the size of all preceding batches. LED indices beyond the configured strip length are ignored by WLED, so padding is safe.

Add 'Update LEDs in batches' setting. When enabled (default, ESP8266 compatible), colours are sent in 54-LED batches. When disabled, all LEDs are sent in a single frame — intended for ESP32 and other controllers with ample heap.
Raise mostly-black frame threshold from 5% to 6.5% to suppress flickering on denser credit frames.
Skip letterbox/pillarbox crop on mostly-black frames to prevent credits from blasting white LEDs.

Fix mock-logging checkbox not rendering (emby-checkbox polyfill skipped it).

Device dropdown now filtered: hides Jellyseerr entries, devices inactive > 2 weeks, and devices last used by login-screen-hidden users.

WebGL: replace ctx.drawImage cross-context copy with gl.readPixels — eliminates the GPU pipeline flush causing ~800 ms per frame on WebOS.

Fixes mock-logging checkbox not rendering in settings.

Performance: 1/4-scale capture and single getImageData call per frame.

Reduces WebGL GPU readback from ~6 MB to ~400 KB; eliminates per-region getImageData calls. Expected to bring refresh rate from ~800 ms to ~50-100 ms on WebOS.

Also fixes the mock-logging checkbox not rendering in the settings page.

Add WebGL capture method setting and mock debug logging toggle.

- New 'Video capture method' setting: Canvas 2D (default) or WebGL for hardware-overlay platforms like WebOS where Canvas 2D returns black pixels.
- New 'Send debug logs to mock WLED server' checkbox, off by default.

Fix: LEDs silent on WebOS/hardware-overlay platforms.

New setting: Video capture method. Select WebGL if the LEDs do not turn on during playback (hardware overlay issue on WebOS/smart TV platforms).
New setting: Send debug logs to mock WLED server (keeps mock log clean by default).
Add plugin icon (shown in Jellyfin's plugin catalogue).

First stable release.

Full-featured WLED edge-lighting plugin for Jellyfin:
- Direct browser → WLED WebSocket connection
- Auto-detects letterbox/pillarbox bars; samples only the actual picture area
- Per-device restriction: limit edge lighting to one specific Jellyfin client
- Inline connection test against the live URL input value
- Clockwise/counter-clockwise strip direction, configurable start position
- Adjustable brightness, sample depth, and update rate
